text.title: Pyramid Principle and SCQA Framework
text.summary: The Pyramid Principle (lead with answer, support with MECE arguments) and SCQA (Situation-Complication-Question-Answer) for building narrative tension in business communication.

rule.statement: Lead with the main point (the answer), then provide supporting arguments, then evidence — never build up to a conclusion.
rule.rationale: Busy decision-makers stop reading the moment their question is answered. Bottom-up structure (background → argument → conclusion) forces them to read everything before knowing whether to care. Pyramid Principle means they can stop at the headline if it answers their question.

example.code:
MAIN POINT: "We should migrate to cloud infrastructure"
ARGUMENT 1: Cost reduction - Evidence: 40% lower TCO analysis - Evidence: Competitor benchmarks
ARGUMENT 2: Scalability - Evidence: Current capacity limits - Evidence: Projected growth
ARGUMENT 3: Speed - Evidence: Deployment time comparison - Evidence: Developer survey

example.note: Bottom-up structure that buries the recommendation.
example.code: "Current infrastructure has high TCO... capacity is limited... deployments take 3 hours... therefore, we should migrate to cloud."

rule.statement: Arguments supporting the main point must be Mutually Exclusive (no overlap) and Collectively Exhaustive (nothing missing).
rule.rationale: Overlapping arguments confuse the audience about whether you have two reasons or one. Missing arguments leave obvious objections unaddressed, which erodes credibility.

section.title: SCQA Framework

rule.statement: Use SCQA when you need to build narrative tension: Situation (shared context), Complication (what changed or threatens), Question (the logical question that arises), Answer (your solution).
rule.rationale: SCQA works because it mirrors how the audience's mind naturally processes a problem. The Complication creates urgency; the Question creates curiosity; the Answer satisfies both. Pure pyramid without SCQA can feel abrupt in presentations.

example.code: S: "Our team ships 10 features per quarter" C: "But 30% have critical bugs in the first week" Q: "How do we maintain velocity while improving quality?" A: "Automated testing with 80% coverage gate"

S: "We dominate the SMB market with 60% share" C: "Enterprise represents 5x larger revenue per customer" Q: "Should we pursue enterprise, and how?" A: "Phased approach: mid-market first, then enterprise"

section.reference: Executive summary template using Pyramid structure
